rationale: The drag-from-tray-to-grid placement gesture maps cleanly onto Bounce Blast's tap-to-place loop and the 25-55 casual relaxation audience overlaps strongly, so the hook framing is reusable. However, frictions are significant: the archetype's bright/light jigsaw palette (#EBEBEB/sky blue/green) clashes with the game's dark moody navy + yellow-orange brand, and a photographic jigsaw image hook misrepresents the actual physics-ball chain-reaction payoff — risking high IPM but poor D1/D7 retention from mismatched expectations. Recommend testing only if we re-skin the hook with the game's actual 3D blocks and pivot the satisfaction beat to the bouncing-ball chain reaction within 3-4 seconds to avoid bait-and-switch penalties.
